root_text.sn48.46:1.2: “Katinaṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.46:1.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti?
root_text.sn48.46:2.2: “dvinnaṁ kho, bhikkhave, indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.46:2.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmīti.
root_text.sn48.46:2.5: Ariyāya ca paññāya, ariyāya ca vimuttiyā.
root_text.sn48.46:2.6: Yā hissa, bhikkhave, ariyā paññā tadassa paññindriyaṁ.
root_text.sn48.46:2.7: Yā hissa, bhikkhave, ariyā vimutti tadassa samādhindriyaṁ.
root_text.sn48.46:2.8: Imesaṁ kho, bhikkhave, dvinnaṁ indriyānaṁ bhāvitattā bahulīkatattā khīṇāsavo bhikkhu aññaṁ byākaroti:
root_text.sn48.46:2.9: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.46:1.2: “Mendicants, how many faculties must a mendicant develop and cultivate so that they can declare enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.46:1.3: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place”’?”
translation_text.sn48.46:2.1: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn48.46:2.2: “A mendicant must develop and cultivate two faculties so that they can declare enlightenment.
translation_text.sn48.46:2.6: For their noble wisdom is the faculty of wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.46:2.7: And their noble freedom is the faculty of immersion.
translation_text.sn48.46:2.8: These are the two faculties that a mendicant must develop and cultivate so that they can declare enlightenment:
translation_text.sn48.46:2.9: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place”’.”
